verdicts.claim: Finding 1: The Panel/ED-137 simple-majority aggregation rule is justified with a fabricated citation â€” dictionaries.py PANEL_CLOSURE, params/contest.md, social_contest_v30.md, and editorial_ledger ED-1057 all claim it 'matches the Â§10 BG parliamentary vote and Â§7.2 Succession plain-majority resolution', but Â§10 (v30:539) is a Mandate-weighted dice pool banded on the track (â‰¥7 passes), not ballot-counting, and Â§7.2 (v30:351) is seven heterogeneous rules (only Crown is a plain-majority-of-Dispositions).
verdicts.reason: Verified against the real files: v30:539 is 'Pool: sum of Mandate ... TN 7' banded on the Persuasion Track (not one-body-one-vote); v30:351 lists 7 distinct aggregation rules (Mandate-weighted / quorum / Disposition / seniority / Favour-weighted / consensus), so neither section establishes plain/simple-majority-of-individual-ballots. The identical fabricated sentence is propagated verbatim to all four surfaces (dictionaries.py:466-469, params/contest.md:201-203, social_contest_v30.md:37/109, ED-1057). Because the underlying aggregation rule is honestly flagged needs_jordan:true, this is a mis-cited RATIONALE, not a silently-injected mechanic â€” major, not blocker. The scope's HARD RULE forbids overruling a fabricated-provenance claim.
verdicts.spawns_ed: Amend ED-1057 + fix the three prose/code surfaces in lockstep: drop the 'Â§10/Â§7.2 plain-majority' claim; state honestly that the corpus institutional-body votes (Â§10 Mandate-weighted pool; Â§7.2 seven heterogeneous power/standing-weighted rules) argue AGAINST importing a weighting scheme onto un-ranked PEER jurors but none fixes the peer-bench case, so simple-majority is a genuinely-undecided peer-bench default (stronger and truthful). Optionally anchor only Â§7.2 Crown 'inner circle by majority Disposition' as the single plain-majority precedent.

verdicts.claim: Finding 2: The ED-137 Panel closure is silently skipped when a caller passes a prebuilt contract.Panel object (rather than the string 'panel'), so Panel reverts to the flat PersuasionTrack/TallyAtClose â€” 'Expert Judge with extra steps', the exact domination the closure claims to prevent. build_contest's else-branch (wrapper.py:144-145) never sets adj_type='panel' for a prebuilt Panel, so the closure branch (wrapper.py:157) never fires; untested.
verdicts.reason: Reproduced: build_contest(5,5,venue='royal_audience',adjudicator=Panel(...)) -> PersuasionTrack; venue='casual_dispute' -> TallyAtClose; both never VoteAtClose. The string path adjudicator='panel' correctly yields VoteAtClose. The wrapper's own comment (lines 154-155) advertises 'or passes a prebuilt Panel with adj_type="panel"' but no code sets it. Kernel tests only exercise the string path (_kernel_tests.py:916), so the gap is untested â€” a documented path silently defeats the ED-137 closure shipped this stage. The finding's crowd-is-Panel caveat is confirmed (isinstance(crowd, Panel)==True), so the fix must not clobber an existing non-None adj_type.
verdicts.spawns_ed: Code fix in build_contest: set adj_type='panel' for a prebuilt contract.Panel ONLY when adj_type is currently None (e.g. `else: the_adj=adjudicator; adj_type = 'panel' if (isinstance(adjudicator, Panel) and adj_type is None) else adj_type`), so crowd (which arrives with adj_type='crowd') keeps PersuasionTrack. Add a _kernel_tests.py case exercising the prebuilt-Panel path.

verdicts.claim: Finding 3: royal_audience and guild_arbitration build byte-identical Venue specs + same adjudicator (expert_judge) and play identically in the current kernel (resistance metadata-only per F10; role_structure pure prose); same collapse for casual_dispute vs personal_appeal.
verdicts.reason: Reproduced byte-identical builds. But the finding itself correctly concedes this is NOT a dictionary defect â€” at the dictionary level the entries are distinct (distinct role_structure/resistance_modifier/flavor) â€” and the resolution-wiring is explicitly scoped OUT this stage (resistance wiring = reserved ED-1055..1079; asymmetric roles = Stage 3+ per the plan). No dictionary change is warranted at Gate B; the proposed remedy is documentation only. A scheduled deferral, not an oversight.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub (contest_rebuild block): record that Royal Audienceâ‰¡Guild Arbitration and Casual Disputeâ‰¡Personal Appeal are mechanically indistinguishable until resistance-modifier (ED-1055..1079) and asymmetric-role (Stage 3) fields are wired, and require a later-stage regression test asserting the pairs diverge once wired.

verdicts.claim: Finding 4: The six Stage-2 typed-dictionary MECHANICS rows (styles_table, interaction_types, derive_interaction, adjudicators_table, faction_boosts, proceedings_table) are marked WIRED but none is consumed in the resolution path â€” they are a parallel authored/player-facing surface, not live in resolution â€” which contradicts the WIRED='live in resolution' contract mechanics_selftest's own docstring states and the PARTIAL precedent the same file set for audience_resistance two commits earlier.
verdicts.reason: Verified zero consumption of all six symbols in resolver.py/modes.py/faction.py/policy.py; they appear only in wrapper's MECHANICS registry, dictionaries.py, tests, and __init__ re-export. mechanics_selftest's docstring explicitly says it 'only vouches for mechanics that are live in resolution', and the same file downgraded audience_resistance WIRED->PARTIAL for being 'DERIVED but not yet plumbed into resolution... metadata-only'. Marking these six WIRED makes the self-test vouch for a liveness that does not exist â€” the precise over-claim the PARTIAL tier was invented to prevent. Minor: the symbols DO resolve to real populated data (no crash/false pass beyond the status label), and 'typed authored surface' is arguably a distinct category from 'derived-but-discarded'.
verdicts.spawns_ed: Downgrade the six rows WIRED->PARTIAL (immediate, matches the audience_resistance precedent) OR introduce a distinct SURFACE status token for authored-typed-surface rows so the WIRED liveness vouch stays honest; add a one-line comment noting the typed surface is authoring/player-facing and cross-checked vs modes.PROCEEDINGS but not itself consumed in resolution.

verdicts.claim: Finding 5: The Formal Contest venue flavor card says 'let the vote fall where the argument lands', but Formal Contest resolves by the Persuasion-Track band (Crowd adjudicator, PersuasionTrack), with no vote/ballot â€” a Lens-6 behavioral-honesty defect. 'vote' is a reserved mechanic (Panel VoteAtClose ballot shipped this stage, Â§10 BG Parliamentary Vote, Â§7.2 Mandate/Favour-weighted vote); the sibling Grand Contest (also Crowd) correctly avoids 'vote'.
verdicts.reason: Reproduced: build_contest(4,4,venue='formal_contest').venue.win is PersuasionTrack (adj=crowd), not VoteAtClose â€” no vote anywhere; the agonist's own kernel test (_kernel_tests.py:925-926) asserts Formal Contest is NOT a VoteAtClose. The card names a mechanism the primitive does not realize and collides with the Panel/VoteAtClose vote mechanic shipped in this same stage, mis-cueing the player toward a ballot resolution; the walkthrough Â§3 resolution screen renders Formal Contest off the track band, contradicting a setup card promising a 'vote'. Grand Contest's vote-free voice makes it an internal inconsistency too. Flavor-text honesty is the explicit Lens-6 focus of Gate B and the scope's HARD RULE forbids overruling dishonest flavor text.
verdicts.spawns_ed: Reword Formal Contest flavor at both surfaces (dictionaries.py:354 + params/contest.md:183) to drop 'vote', e.g. 'Three rounds before the assembly. Take turns, win the room, and let the track fall where the argument lands.' Optionally harden _kernel_tests.py:897-900 to assert no non-Panel proceeding flavor contains 'vote'/'ballot'.

open_decisions: Panel aggregation rule (the genuine design call ED-1057 flags, independent of the Finding-1 citation fix): confirm SIMPLE MAJORITY (implemented, >half the ballots, tie->draw) as the Panel verdict rule, or select WEIGHTED-BY-STANDING (needs a per-juror standing attribute the Panel type does not yet carry) or UNANIMITY-REQUIRED (would make Panel strictly harder to win than a single Expert Judge â€” a dominance asymmetry). Swappable in one line at dictionaries.panel_win_condition(). NOTE: the corpus does NOT ground any of these for a peer bench (Â§10 is a Mandate-weighted pool, Â§7.2 is seven heterogeneous power/standing-weighted rules), so this is a genuinely-undecided invention â€” see Finding 1.

MECHANICS status taxonomy (Finding 4): decide whether authored typed-dictionary/data rows that are player-/authoring-facing but not consumed in resolution should get a dedicated SURFACE status token, or simply be marked PARTIAL like the audience_resistance precedent. Affects what mechanics_selftest's WIRED liveness vouch is allowed to cover.

verdicts.claim: Finding 7: Royal Audience venue flavor calls the single Expert Judge "The bench" (dictionaries.py:367, mirrored params/contest.md:185), but "bench" is the canon term reserved for the multi-judge Panel adjudicator (v30:37; and used exclusively for Panel everywhere else in dictionaries.py). The card cues the wrong adjudicator cardinality â€” a deliberating multi-judge body â€” for a proceeding that is one authority.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed against real files: modes.PROCEEDINGS['royal_audience'].adjudicator=='expert_judge' (a single authority, v30:34), yet its flavor reads 'The bench is stern' on both single-sourced surfaces (dictionaries.py:367 + params/contest.md:185). 'bench'/'Bench of individual judges' is canonically the Panel term (v30:37; params:22,194) and is used for Panel exclusively throughout dictionaries.py. This is dishonest flavor per Lens 6 (adjudicator-cardinality mis-cue) â€” the hard rule forbids overruling dishonest flavor. Fix is a simple reword ('The judge is stern'/'The one who hears you is stern') on both surfaces, NOT a design fork, so no Jordan call needed. The existing Lens-6 guard at _kernel_tests.py:905-906 catches vote/ballot but not bench/panel/judges(plural); extend it.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: Reword Royal Audience flavor to name a single authority (not 'bench') at dictionaries.py:367 + params/contest.md:185 (single-sourced); extend the _kernel_tests.py:905 Lens-6 proceeding-flavor guard to assert no NON-Panel proceeding flavor contains 'bench'/'panel'/'judges'(plural) so the adjudicator-cardinality mis-cue cannot regress.

verdicts.claim: Finding 3: The ED-137 Panel closure wires real VoteAtClose mechanics to the 'panel' adjudicator, but NO canonical proceeding instantiates Panel, so the entire closed mechanic is unreachable through normal play (dead entry per the churn axiom). The v30 Panel row's Examples column was overwritten with the mechanism description, so Panel has no in-world proceeding example AND no proceeding wiring.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: `[k for k,s in modes.PROCEEDINGS.items() if s['adjudicator']=='panel']` == [] â€” all 8 proceedings route to crowd/expert_judge/no_adjudicator. Panel is instantiable ONLY via explicit adjudicator='panel' to build_contest, never by selecting a canonical proceeding. v30:37 Examples column reads 'Bench of individual judges â†’ terminal per-member VoteAtClose ballot' (a mechanism description) while the other three adjudicator rows (v30:34-36) name real in-world situations. Empirical distinctness confirmed (panel yields binary a/b 1176/824 vs expert_judge's committee/decisive band 1883/100/16/1 over 2000 seeded runs) â€” so Panel is non-dominated but still DEAD. Closing ED-137 mechanically is not the same as making Panel reachable. Binding Panel to a proceeding (which one? a bench-tried tribunal variant? split Church Tribunal?) vs restoring an in-world Examples entry is a genuine design-authority call.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: Make the closed Panel mechanic reachable â€” either (a) bind Panel to a canonical proceeding (natural home: a multi-judge tribunal/appellate variant, or split Church Tribunal into single-Inquisitor vs full-Panel forms) authored via the ledger, OR (b) restore a real in-world Examples entry for the Panel row (v30 Â§2 Step 1) naming the situation(s) that trigger a Panel plus a proceeding/flag that routes to it. Design fork â€” needs_jordan.

verdicts.claim: Finding 4: The two Obscuring Styles (Suppression, Insinuation) are strictly dominated by their Revealing genre-mates (Precedent, Vision) in single-exchange contests â€” an Obscuring win produces zero track movement and only a Doubt Marker whose sole effect (-2 to the opponent's NEXT winning exchange, consumed on use) has EV exactly 0 when there is no next exchange, violating the churn axiom's 'every style ever correct somewhere'.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed against canon: v30:180-183 â€” an Obscuring win does NOT move the Persuasion Track; it places a Doubt Marker reducing the opponent's next winning margin by 2 (consumed on use, min 0). modes.PROCEEDINGS exchange counts confirm casual_dispute (1,1) and personal_appeal (1,1) are always single-exchange, and church_tribunal (1,5)/private_negotiation (1,3) can run length-1. In every such case the Doubt Marker cannot fire, so an Obscuring win forgoes the sole track/tally movement for nothing â€” strictly worse than the Revealing genre-mate at equal successes. Note: the resolver does not yet consume orientation (Stage-3 armature scope), so this is a design-table dominance the Stage-2 dictionary+flavor commit to, not a current-kernel bug â€” but it is a real dead/dominated choice. Resolution (terminal-valued marker vs gating Obscuring out of short proceedings) is a genuine design fork.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: Remove the single-exchange dominance of Obscuring styles â€” either give the Doubt Marker a non-zero terminal value (in a single/final-exchange contest apply its -2 to the closing tally/track band directly, or convert to a small immediate track effect), OR gate Obscuring style selection out of single-exchange proceedings at the dictionary level and document why. Design fork â€” needs_jordan; resolve before the Obscuring flavor is treated as behaviorally honest (couples to finding 5).

verdicts.claim: Finding 5: Suppression/Insinuation flavor promises a downstream Doubt-Marker payoff ('their next point', 'if they win') that provably cannot fire in single-exchange proceedings; and the Insinuation code comment (dictionaries.py:122-125) asserts 'self-Face backfire risk (CR5, Stage 3)' which is explicitly NOT wired and is not surfaced in the player-facing string.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: dictionaries.py:106 (Suppression 'Leave a doubt where their next point should land') and :126 (Insinuation 'Imply what follows if they win') both name a downstream consequence that cannot occur in a (1,1) proceeding â€” dishonest exactly in the finding-4 dominated case. Separately, v30:456 states the CR5 Face-attack/self-backfire 'is NOT yet realized' and no player-facing string mentions it, so the Insinuation comment claims a Stage-3 lever the card does not present. Copy/comment only, not resolution logic, hence minor â€” but it compounds finding 4 by selling a dominated line as strategically live.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: After finding-4 resolution, re-word the two Obscuring flavors so the promised effect matches what actually fires for the proceeding the card is shown for (or add a proceeding-aware note that the Doubt Marker needs a later exchange); and drop the 'self-Face backfire' claim from the Insinuation comment (dictionaries.py:122-125) since CR5 is unwired this stage â€” flavor/comments must not advertise a Stage-3 lever.

verdicts.claim: Finding 1: ED-1057's ledger description (editorial_ledger.jsonl:816) lists code symbols 'PANEL_VOTE_SHARPNESS/NOISE [SEED]' in dictionaries.py, but those symbols exist nowhere â€” dictionaries.py deliberately leaves sharpness/noise at VoteAtClose's constructor defaults (line 535, with an anti-fabrication rationale). The ledger over-claims two intentionally-omitted code artifacts.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: grep for PANEL_VOTE_SHARPNESS/PANEL_VOTE_NOISE across the tree returns only ledger:816 itself â€” no code definition. dictionaries.py:535 confirms 'sharpness/noise left at VoteAtClose's own cited [SEED] defaults'. PANEL_AGGREGATION, PANEL_DEFAULT_JURORS, panel_win_condition() do exist. Direction of error is benign (code is cleaner than the ledger), not a fabricated numeric value, but an auditor tracing ED-1057 would hunt for non-existent symbols.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub: Edit the ED-1057 description in editorial_ledger.jsonl:816 to drop 'PANEL_VOTE_SHARPNESS/NOISE [SEED]' and replace with a truthful note that sharpness/noise are single-sourced at resolver.VoteAtClose's cited [SEED] constructor defaults, not re-typed in dictionaries.py; re-validate the JSONL.

verdicts.claim: Finding 2: editorial_ledger.jsonl:137 still carries the 2026-04-04 ED-137 row verbatim (status resolved, 'Full panel mechanics deferred to Contest System v3'), while every prose surface (v30:624, params/contest.md) now says ED-137 is CLOSED via VoteAtClose â€” the machine ledger reads as still-deferred.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: editorial_ledger.jsonl:137 unchanged ('deferred to Contest System v3'); v30:624 reads 'ED-137 ... CLOSED (Stage 2 / Gate B, ED-1057)'. This is consistent with the corpus new-ED-supersedes convention (ED-1057 supersedes ED-137), so it is not a fabricated value â€” but the stale JSONL decision text is now contradicted by every prose surface. No numeric value at stake.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub (optional, matches supersession convention): either leave as-is (ED-1057 is the live superseding row) or add a one-clause 'superseded by ED-1057 (closure)' note to the JSONL ED-137 decision so the machine ledger does not read as still-deferred.

verdicts.claim: Finding 6: resolver.VoteAtClose defaults jurors=7 (resolver.py:105) while dictionaries.PANEL_DEFAULT_JURORS reads modes.panel(size=5)=>5 and the groundup deliberative_body presets default 7 â€” three different 'default panel size' values coexist in one package.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: PANEL_DEFAULT_JURORS==5, VoteAtClose jurors default==7, modes.panel size default==5, groundup presets==7. No live defect on the canonical path (panel_win_condition() builds jurors=5, and a paired contract.Panel's member count overrides via len(members) at resolve). Purely a latent convention smell: a future direct VoteAtClose() call in the canonical package would silently pick up 7, not 5. Latent, not a naming collision of canonical state names â€” hence nit, not overruled.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED stub (optional, not required for Gate B): single-source the one bench-size [SEED] literal so resolver.VoteAtClose's default and modes.panel's default cannot drift (shared PANEL_DEFAULT_SIZE), or add a kernel-test asserting VoteAtClose().jurors == modes.panel().members count for the canonical panel path.

open_decisions: PANEL AGGREGATION RULE (the ED-137 closure judgment call, already flagged by the build as ED-1057 needs_jordan:true): confirm SIMPLE MAJORITY (a) â€” implemented, VoteAtClose votesA*2>n â€” or select (b) weighted-by-standing or (c) unanimity-required. The corpus does NOT settle this for a peer bench: Â§10 (v30:539) is a Mandate-weighted dice pool and Â§7.2 (v30:351) is seven heterogeneous rules, so the corpus argues AGAINST importing a standing WEIGHT onto un-ranked peer jurors but fixes no peer-bench rule; Â§7.2 Crown 'inner circle by majority Disposition' is the one plain-majority-of-individuals analogue. Simple majority is the best-grounded undecided default; swap is a one-line edit at dictionaries.panel_win_condition(). Provenance overclaim already corrected by the build (no fabricated Â§10/Â§7.2 plain-majority precedent is asserted).

PANEL REACHABILITY (finding 3, upheld major): closing ED-137 mechanically shipped a Panel mechanic that is non-dominated (binary a/b verdict vs Expert Judge's committee/decisive band â€” empirically distinct) but DEAD â€” no canonical proceeding routes to it, and the v30 Panel row's Examples column was overwritten with the mechanism description. Decide how Panel becomes reachable: (a) bind it to a canonical proceeding (a multi-judge tribunal/appellate variant, or split Church Tribunal into single-Inquisitor vs full-Panel forms) authored via the ledger, or (b) restore a real in-world Examples entry naming the situation(s) that trigger a Panel plus a proceeding/flag that routes to it. As-is, 'Panel closes ED-137' ships an inert mechanic the churn axiom flags.

OBSCURING-STYLE SINGLE-EXCHANGE DOMINANCE (finding 4, upheld major): Suppression and Insinuation are strictly dominated by Precedent and Vision in single-exchange proceedings (Casual Dispute, Personal Appeal, and length-1 Church Tribunal/Private Negotiation) because the Doubt Marker's -2 (opponent's next winning exchange, consumed on use) has EV 0 when there is no next exchange. Genuine design fork: (a) give the Doubt Marker a non-zero TERMINAL value (apply its -2 to the closing tally/track band, or convert to a small immediate track effect on a single/final exchange), or (b) gate Obscuring style selection out of single-exchange proceedings at the dictionary level and document why. Resolve before the Obscuring flavor (finding 5) can be treated as behaviorally honest.

verdicts.claim: Terminal Doubt rule (ED-1060) is directionally self-contradictory: '-2 ... in the marked side's favour' vs 'slides ... toward the Obscuring winner' name OPPOSITE beneficiaries; literal reading rewards the Obscuring LOSER, re-introducing/worsening the dominance the fix was meant to cure. Present identically in dictionaries.py:144/:160, params/contest.md:110, social_contest_v30.md:184 (and ledger ED-1060).
verdicts.reason: Confirmed by tracing referents against canon: v30:180 places the marker 'on the opponent' (of the Obscuring winner) and v30:181's normal effect reduces the MARKED side's next winning margin, i.e. the marker works AGAINST the marked side / IN THE PLANTER'S (Obscuring winner's) favour. The Terminal clause's 'in the marked side's favour' therefore reverses the marker (benefits the party who LOST the Obscuring exchange), while the very same sentence's 'toward the Obscuring winner' names the correct planter beneficiary. The two clauses are mutually contradictory and the literal reading makes an Obscuring win in a single/final exchange strictly self-harmful (worse than EV 0), strengthening the exact Suppression<Precedent / Insinuation<Vision dominance ED-1060 exists to cure. This is a wording/direction correction, not a design fork, so it must be fixed on all four surfaces before Gate B is clean; the a-vs-b design fork remains separately open for Jordan. Not a no-repro: the referent trace is exact and reproducible.

verdicts.claim: In the CURRENT resolution path Royal Audience is mechanically identical to Guild Arbitration, and Casual Dispute to Personal Appeal; their only distinguishing dictionary fields (resistance_modifier, role_structure) are un-plumbed metadata, so four of eight venues collapse to two indistinguishable pairs.
verdicts.reason: Verified: proceeding_venue() (modes.py:459-469) reads only exchanges/track_start/tracker; resistance is metadata-only (wrapper.py:71-75, F10) and roles are unread, so royal_audience and guild_arbitration produce byte-identical Venue+adjudicator (budget 3 / expert_judge / PersuasionTrack 5.0 / required). Casual Dispute vs Personal Appeal differ in tracker_mode ('none' vs 'optional' â€” Personal Appeal alone can opt-in via use_tracker), so that pair is NOT strictly identical, but at default they resolve the same. However the finding itself proposes NO Stage-2 dictionary change (the typed surface correctly carries the distinct labels) and the un-plumbed state is already honestly disclosed (wrapper F10 + reserved ED contest_rebuild 1055-1079). The distinguishing channels (resistance F10, CR5 roles/orientation) are explicitly out of Stage-2 scope. Deferred to the reserved ED stub as the specific Stage-3 regression targets; not a defect in the Stage-2 dictionaries.
verdicts.spawns_ed: contest_rebuild reserved ED (1055-1079): when resistance (F10) and CR5 roles/orientation are plumbed, re-check that royal_audience/guild_arbitration and casual_dispute/personal_appeal resolve non-identically (churn axiom: all 8 venues mechanically distinct once wired).

verdicts.claim: Guilds faction-boost row stores the GM-framed literal 'GM picks' as a typed boost value (FACTION_BOOSTS['guilds'].boost, dictionaries.py:348), un-routed and un-flagged, and _kernel_tests.py:889 codifies 'GM picks' as a licit boost â€” embedding an unresolved GM at the data layer, violating the GM-removal mandate (Lens 7), unlike the two other Stage-2 forks which the agonist flagged.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed: dictionaries.py:348 boost='GM picks', axis='Either'; _kernel_tests.py:889 asserts 'GM picks' is a valid boost; grep finds no GM-removal note/resolver/ED for it. This is a genuine double standard against modes.py:309-312, which explicitly GM-removes the Â§2 'GM setup' choices (adjudicator type, exchange count, resistance, track start) into venue-intrinsic parameters. Every other boost row is a real axis value; 'GM picks' is the sole un-routed GM decision embedded in the engine surface. The remedy is correct that the params VALUE must NOT be silently rewritten in place (oracle-correction hazard) â€” it must be flagged exactly as the Panel-reachability and Terminal-Doubt forks were flagged, with an ED and an engine-resolver candidate, and the kernel test should stop asserting 'GM picks' as licit until a resolver exists. Upheld as a flag-and-ED action, not a value change; the resolver rule itself is a Jordan design call.
verdicts.spawns_ed: GUILDS_BOOST_OPEN (provisional/needs_jordan): Guilds' 'GM picks one' (params Â§Faction Boosts / social_contest Â§2 Step 3) is an un-routed GM-setup decision needing an engine resolver (e.g. engine selects the axis the arguing side is not already boosted on, or a scene-context rule). Add the flag in dictionaries.py, keep the verbatim canon string in a provenance field, and stop asserting 'GM picks' as a licit boost in _kernel_tests.py:889 until the resolver exists.

verdicts.claim: New canonical term 'Terminal Doubt' was not registered in references/glossary.md or references/mechanical_terms_index.md, unlike its sibling 'Doubt Marker' which is in both â€” a term-registry consistency gap.
verdicts.reason: Verified: 'Doubt Marker' is registered (glossary.md:83, mechanical_terms_index.md:611); 'Terminal Doubt' appears in references/ only as a canonical_sources.yaml comment. Genuine gap, but correctly rated a nit because those registries demonstrably lag (glossary still lists retired 'Composure'; CR3 'Face' absent), so registration is a loosely-followed convention, not a hard gate â€” and 'Terminal Doubt' is itself provisional/needs_jordan (ED-1060). Deferred to ED-1060 ratification: add the row alongside 'Doubt Marker' when the mechanic settles.
verdicts.spawns_ed: On ED-1060 ratification: add a 'Terminal Doubt' row to references/mechanical_terms_index.md and references/glossary.md (source: contest Â§4), optionally sweeping the stale 'Composure' (retire) / missing 'Face' (add) entries in the same pass.

verdicts.claim: Private Negotiation flavor ('Keep score if you both agree to') invents a two-party mutual-consent gate on the Persuasion Track that does not exist in canon or the kernel â€” a Lens-6 fabrication-class honesty break.
verdicts.reason: Confirmed against canon: v30:76 makes the tracker simply OPTIONAL ('If not used, winner determined by exchange majority') with NO mutual-consent language; grep confirms canon nowhere pairs the tracker with 'both agree'/'mutual'/'consent' (the sole 'mutual' hit is unrelated 'mutual stonewalling'). Mechanically the toggle is a single build-time use_tracker flag (modes.py:444-457; wrapper.py), never an in-fiction action by either party, and in a no-GM engine it is a venue/build parameter. So 'if you both agree to' presents a non-existent in-fiction negotiation between the two orators as a live mechanic â€” a behaviorally dishonest card. Cross-check: Personal Appeal (identical tracker_mode='optional') makes no such claim, so the two optional-tracker venues are inconsistent and only Private Negotiation overclaims. Must be reworded (both single-sourced surfaces: dictionaries.py:433 + params/contest.md:191) before Gate B is clean; the honest remainder ('failing to agree is its own answer' = the v30:76 stall) can stay.

verdicts.claim: Church Tribunal flavor 'the room already leans against you' cues a Crowd/audience, but Church Tribunal's adjudicator is a single Expert Judge and the 'lean' is a Persuasion-Track start bias (start 6), not an audience disposition â€” a softer instance of the adjudicator-cardinality mis-cue class the finding-7 fix addressed.
verdicts.reason: Verified: modes.PROCEEDINGS['church_tribunal'].adjudicator=='expert_judge' (a single authority, v30:34), not a Crowd; the mechanical bias is CHURCH_TRIBUNAL_TRACK_START=6.0 (v30:343 'Persuasion Track starts biased at 6'), a track-start position, not a leaning room. 'the room' idiomatically implies the Crowd adjudicator (the one that 'reacts to delivery and force', v30:35), which the Tribunal does not convene. Genuine mis-cue, but rated minor and not overruled: it is defensible-as-idiom (a tribunal has a 'room'), the kernel guard (_kernel_tests.py:912) is satisfied by 'halved' so the phrasing is freely swappable, and the fix (e.g. '...the ground/case already leans against you') tightens the card to the single-Expert-Judge reality consistent with the finding-7 fix. Upheld as a recommended tightening; not Gate-B-blocking on its own.

open_decisions: PANEL AGGREGATION RULE (ED-1057, the flagged Panel/ED-137 aggregation call): confirm SIMPLE MAJORITY (implemented default, PANEL_AGGREGATION='simple_majority', the natural one-judge-one-vote peer-bench rule the corpus leaves undecided) OR select (b) weighted-by-standing (needs a per-juror standing attribute the Panel type does not yet carry) OR (c) unanimity-required (would make Panel strictly harder to win than Expert Judge â€” a dominance asymmetry). Swap is a one-line edit at dictionaries.panel_win_condition().

PANEL REACHABILITY (ED-1059): no canonical proceeding routes to Panel â€” it is selectable only via explicit adjudicator='panel'. Decide how Panel becomes proceeding-selectable: (a) add/bind a Panel-bearing proceeding (appellate/high-tribunal variant, or split Church Tribunal into single-Inquisitor vs full-Panel) â€” changes the fixed 8-proceeding roster; OR (b) an appeal/escalation flag that routes an existing proceeding to a bench â€” roster intact. Currently neither is wired (8-proceeding invariant preserved; in-world example restored only).

TERMINAL DOUBT rule (ED-1060) a-vs-b: confirm (a) the terminal-value rule (unconsumed Doubt Marker at close applies its -2 once) OR (b) gate Obscuring style-selection out of single-exchange proceedings entirely. INDEPENDENTLY of this fork, the synthesist must first correct the directional self-contradiction in the (a) wording (finding 1) on all four surfaces before either option is coherent â€” the -2 must consistently favour the Obscuring winner (planter) / go AGAINST the marked side, matching the normal marker.

GUILDS 'GM picks' resolver rule (new GUILDS_BOOST_OPEN ED): the Guilds faction boosts 'GM picks one' axis â€” in a no-GM engine this needs an engine resolver. Decide the rule (e.g. engine picks the axis the arguing side is NOT already boosted on, or a scene-context-driven pick). Until decided, flag it (do not rewrite the params value) and stop asserting 'GM picks' as a licit boost in the kernel test.

verdicts.claim: Finding 1 (major): the flagship non-domination fix (Terminal Doubt, ED-1060) is written entirely in Persuasion-Track 'margin/band/Compromise-zone' language, but 2 of the 4 single-exchange proceedings it names as motivating cases (Casual Dispute â€” always TallyAtClose; Personal Appeal â€” TallyAtClose by default) resolve by exchange-majority TallyAtClose, which returns raw A/B/draw with no bands and no Compromise zone, so the rule has nothing to operate on in exactly those proceedings.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED by repro: proceeding_venue() shows casual_dispute=TallyAtClose, personal_appeal=TallyAtClose, church_tribunal=PersuasionTrack; TallyAtClose.resolve (resolver.py:60-64) returns only A/B/draw. The ED-1060 text on all three surfaces (dictionaries.py:160-166, params/contest.md:110, social_contest_v30.md:184) specifies the terminal marker as 'âˆ’2 subtracted from the closing margin/band' and 'slides a Compromise-zone result one step' â€” constructs that exist ONLY on PersuasionTrack. So the churn-axiom repair is well-defined only for Church Tribunal (the sole banded case among the four named); for the TallyAtClose subset the design-table commitment is undefined, leaving Suppression/Insinuation still potentially dominated there. The agonist's 'design-table commitment only, resolver does not yet consume orientation, Stage-3 scope' defense does not dissolve this â€” the design commitment ITSELF is incoherent for half its own named cases. Not a resolution-number change to fix: honestly define what a terminal marker does under raw A/B tally (flip / no-flip of adv), or adopt alternative (b) gate-Obscuring-out precisely for the tally subset. Couples to the genuine (a)-vs-(b) fork already at needs_jordan.
verdicts.spawns_ed: ED-1060 addendum: split the Terminal Doubt rule by resolution mechanism â€” specify separately (i) the banded PersuasionTrack behavior (already written) and (ii) the TallyAtClose behavior for Casual Dispute (always) / Personal Appeal / Private Negotiation-at-length-1 (either 'the terminal âˆ’2 applies to the raw adv difference against the marked side, min 0' or 'gate Obscuring out of the tally subset'); add a DOUBT_MARKER field distinguishing banded-vs-tally terminal behavior and a guard that both are separately specified.

verdicts.claim: Findings 2+3 (minor, consolidated): the Guilds faction boost is carried verbatim as boost='GM picks', axis='Either' (dictionaries.py:349-352) into the freshly-authored typed FACTION_BOOSTS dict with no GM-removal flag, no dedicated ED, and no resolver hook â€” the single un-flagged No-GM-mandate seam in a surface this stage authored, while the build filed explicit flagged EDs + inline OPEN-DECISION flags for its two other seams (DOUBT_MARKER=ED-1060, Panel=ED-1057/1059).
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED. dictionaries.py:351 carries boost='GM picks'/axis='Either' with only a transcription comment; ED-1058 records it as a verbatim data-transcription note, NOT as a flagged GM-removal violation with a remedy path; name_collision_database.yaml:464 already flags 'GM' as a stale TTRPG term that 'contradicts No-GM identity â€” flag for removal'; modes.py:309-312 applies the GM-removal mandate to every OTHER Â§2 GM-setup decision (made venue-intrinsic). The value is faithful to the oracle (params/contest.md:73), so this is NOT a fabricated constant and the fix must NOT rewrite params in place (that would be oracle-correction-in-place, a blocker). But it is a real un-flagged No-GM seam in a fresh surface and a latent dead/ambiguous entry when Stage 3 wires boosts, so per Lens 7 (GM-removal mandate) and the hard rule (do not overrule a dead/dominated entry) it stands. Remedy is low-cost and correctly scoped: an inline OPEN-DECISION-FOR-JORDAN flag on the Guilds FactionBoost mirroring DOUBT_MARKER/PANEL_CLOSURE, plus a provisional/needs_jordan ED against canon.
verdicts.spawns_ed: New provisional/needs_jordan ED (contest_rebuild block): flag the stale 'GM picks'/'Either' Guilds value in params Â§Faction Boosts + social_contest Â§2 Step 3 for GM-removal; in a no-GM engine the Guilds either-axis boost must resolve via an engine rule (e.g. the orator's chosen axis, the faction's ethical-mode disposition, or a seeded/max-bonus pick) â€” flagged as OPEN DECISION FOR JORDAN, not decided unilaterally. Add the inline flag to dictionaries.FACTION_BOOSTS['guilds'].

verdicts.claim: Finding 4 (minor): the two asymmetric-proceeding Venue flavors (Royal Audience, Church Tribunal) are hardcoded to the disadvantaged party's POV ('You petition'; 'the room already leans against you') and become behaviorally dishonest when the player occupies the advantaged role, which build_contest permits.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED. build_contest(side_a, side_b, ...) (wrapper.py:99, _as_contestant/positional assignment) enforces no petitioner/accused role on side_a; the single fixed per-proceeding flavor string is POV-locked (Royal Audience 'the throne's resistance is halved for the one who comes asking' + 'You petition'; Church Tribunal 'leans against you' + 'Halved resistance is the only mercy'), and per social_contest_v30.md:339/343 the halved resistance + biased track favour the accused/petitioner â€” so if the player is the Crown/Inquisitor (advantaged, and permitted as either side) the card describes the OPPONENT's benefit and 'You petition' is simply false. Latent for the eventual UI (dictionaries store one string; no per-side rendering in the sim yet), hence minor, but it is a real behavioral-honesty gap the kernel guard does not cover (_kernel_tests only asserts the card contains 'halved'/'lean').
verdicts.spawns_ed: No new ED required; fold into the Stage-2 dictionary ED as a documented POV-lock assumption: annotate the two asymmetric-proceeding flavors in dictionaries.py that they are authored from the canonical player-role POV (petitioner/accused) so a Godot port does not render them to the advantaged seat, OR neutralize the POV to name both roles; add a kernel guard that asymmetric-proceeding flavor either names both roles or is flagged POV-locked.

verdicts.claim: Finding 6 (nit): Church Tribunal flavor 'every doubt they plant is meant to stick' over-claims Doubt Marker durability â€” mechanically a Doubt Marker is one-shot (consumed on use, only one active at a time), and the Church's actual edge is the Obscuring boost (markers land more OFTEN), not durability.
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED. social_contest_v30.md:182-183 'Only one Doubt Marker active at a time. New replaces old.' + 'Consumed on use.' â€” non-persistent, one-shot; :343 'Church boosts Obscuring' is the real mechanical edge (frequency, not persistence). 'meant to stick' cues a durability/persistence mechanic that does not exist â€” behaviorally-dishonest flavor, which the hard rule explicitly says not to overrule. Fix is a small reword naming the frequency edge (Obscuring boost) rather than durability.
verdicts.spawns_ed: No ED; single-line flavor reword in dictionaries.PROCEEDINGS_TABLE['church_tribunal'].flavor + params/contest.md:188 (single-sourced), e.g. name the Obscuring-boost frequency edge instead of 'meant to stick'.

verdicts.claim: Finding 5 (nit): none of the 8 Venue flavors are verb-first, contradicting the scope's explicit CDS-voice 'active, verb-first' requirement for both Styles AND Venues (the 4 Style flavors ARE verb-first; all 8 Venues begin with a numeral/article/pronoun).
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED by repro: every Venue flavor's first token is a non-verb (Three/Five/You/The/A/One/Just/One). The scope prompt lists 'verb-first' as a requirement for both Styles and Venues, and ED-1058's own description claims the flavor is 'active/verb-first' â€” which is verifiably false for all 8 venues, so this touches flavor honesty (the ED over-claims), not just style, and is not overrulable. Genuine nit: either recast the Venue openers verb-first, or make the Style-vs-Venue voice split (imperative action-cards vs descriptive setup-cards, per walkthrough Â§1) an explicit, documented decision rather than a silent inconsistency â€” and correct the ED-1058 'verb-first' claim to match.
verdicts.spawns_ed: No new ED; correct the ED-1058 description's 'verb-first' claim for venues, and either recast the 8 Venue openers verb-first or record the setup-card-vs-action-card voice split as an explicit documented decision in dictionaries.py.

verdicts.claim: Finding 7 (nit): inconsistent tracker-optionality signalling â€” Private Negotiation (optional) flags 'no fixed scorekeeping' but Personal Appeal (also optional) gives no scorekeeping cue ('no one to referee it'), while Casual Dispute (none) says 'no scorekeeping' (absolute).
verdicts.reason: CONFIRMED by repro: casual_dispute (tracker_mode none) says 'no scorekeeping'; private_negotiation (optional) says 'no fixed scorekeeping'; personal_appeal (optional) says nothing about scorekeeping. The optionality is honestly conveyed for one optional venue and omitted for the other â€” a consistency/completeness gap, not a fabrication (the finding itself concedes silence is not a false claim), hence lowest priority. Fix is a parallel optionality cue on Personal Appeal.
verdicts.spawns_ed: No ED; add a parallel scorekeeping/optionality cue to dictionaries.PROCEEDINGS_TABLE['personal_appeal'].flavor + params/contest.md:192, consistent with Private Negotiation.

open_decisions: PANEL AGGREGATION RULE (ED-1057, the primary flagged fork): ED-137 is closed with Panel = a bench of individual judges deliberating to a terminal VoteAtClose per-member ballot; the aggregation is implemented as SIMPLE MAJORITY (best-grounded: the corpus fixes no plain-majority rule for a peer bench, but its institutional votes â€” Â§10 Mandate-weighted, Â§7.2's seven heterogeneous rules â€” argue AGAINST importing a standing WEIGHT onto un-ranked peer jurors, and Â§7.2 Crown 'majority Disposition' is the closest one-judge-one-vote analogue; needs no invented per-juror attribute; keeps Panel non-dominated vs Expert Judge). Confirm SIMPLE MAJORITY, or select (b) weighted-by-standing (needs a per-juror standing attribute the Panel type does not carry) / (c) unanimity-required (would make Panel strictly harder to win than Expert Judge). One-line swap at dictionaries.panel_win_condition().

PANEL REACHABILITY (ED-1059): the closed Panel mechanic is currently reachable ONLY via explicit adjudicator='panel' â€” none of the 8 canonical proceedings routes to Panel (a dead-through-normal-play entry per the churn axiom; the in-world example is restored but no proceeding SELECTS Panel). Choose: (a) add/bind a Panel-bearing proceeding (appellate/high-tribunal variant, or split Church Tribunal into single-Inquisitor vs full-Panel â€” changes the fixed 8-proceeding roster), or (b) an appeal/escalation flag that routes an existing proceeding to Panel (roster intact). Neither is wired.

TERMINAL DOUBT (ED-1060) â€” TWO coupled sub-decisions: (i) the base (a)-vs-(b) fork: give an unconsumed Doubt Marker a non-zero terminal value at contest close (implemented) vs gate Obscuring out of single-exchange proceedings; AND (ii, from upheld Finding 1) how the terminal marker behaves under TallyAtClose (Casual Dispute always; Personal Appeal / Private Negotiation at length 1), where there is no band/Compromise-zone for the current 'slides one step' rule to operate on â€” define the raw-A/B-tally treatment or gate Obscuring out of the tally subset specifically. The current rule cures dominance only for the banded Church Tribunal among the four named cases.

GUILDS 'GM picks'/'Either' BOOST (upheld Findings 2+3): the value is faithful to params/contest.md Â§Faction Boosts but is a No-GM-mandate seam in the newly-authored typed dict. In a no-GM engine the Guilds either-axis boost must resolve by an ENGINE rule, not a GM. Decide the resolution: the orator's chosen axis, the faction's ethical-mode disposition, a seeded pick, or the axis maximizing the acting orator's bonus. (Do NOT rewrite params in place â€” route through a provisional ED.)

FACE SCALE-BINDING & COMPOSURE BLAST-RADIUS (carried from Gate A, still needs_jordan, ED-1056): confirm the Face_current = round(Standing Ã· 10 Ã— Face_max) combo formula and the scoped-rename-only Composure retirement (contest tracker only; knots/combat/conviction untouched). Adjacent to Stage 2 because dictionaries surface Face-bearing venues.

VENUE FLAVOR VOICE (upheld Finding 5, authorial nit): confirm whether the 8 Venue setup-cards should be recast verb-first to match the scope's stated CDS voice, or the Style-imperative-vs-Venue-descriptive split is accepted as an explicit documented decision (and ED-1058's 'verb-first' claim corrected accordingly).
